memories.content: User requested a deep check on the last 12 hours. Assistant performed a discovery search via Hacker News and web, produced a report highlighting one warm contact (roserugco on AI memory plugins) and limitations (Reddit blocked, DuckDuckGo rate limited), and offered to write a comment or use Exa API.

Discovery run on 2026-07-02 14:49 UTC, last 12 hours. Sources checked: Hacker News (via Algolia API), web search (partial - DuckDuckGo rate limited). Reddit blocked (403). Key findings: 1) playorizaya - Ask HN about AI talent hoarding (hot by scoring, but philosophical) 2) roserugco - Ask HN about AI memory plugins (practical agent user) 3) quirk - Ask HN about managing AI artifacts (using Cursor agents). No direct "I need to build an AI agent" posts found in timeframe.
запусти, проверку в глубину на последние 12 часов.
User requested a demo. Assistant activated demo mode and presented the created knowledge base files, a lead scoring script, and a 5-stage pipeline.
ru
demo
true
Assistant introduced itself, explained it is unconfigured, and offered two onboarding modes: Demo mode (pre-configured to sell AI agents) or Custom mode (interview to tailor).
User greeted, and assistant responded by expressing lack of configuration and asking onboarding questions about business, ideal client, and signal.

Workspace в начальном состоянии: отсутствуют tools/score.py, skills/scout/SKILL.md, config/icp.json, knowledge/service.md. Есть только корневые .md файлы (IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md, AGENTS.md и др.), папка state/ с пустыми JSON-заглушками и папка rules/. Требуется онбординг.
Пользователь запросил проверку существования и первых строк четырёх файлов через file_read. Ассистент сообщил, что все четыре файла не существуют, а workspace находится в начальном состоянии.
The /data/workspace/tools/ directory is empty; scripts profile.py, score.py, and state.py do not exist. Only state/*.json files are present.
The assistant executed shell commands to inspect the workspace and run tools, discovering that the required Python scripts (profile.py, score.py, state.py) are missing from /data/workspace/tools/.
User is setting up the 'scout' skill for prospect discovery via web search.
User requested execution of a shell command to create the directory /data/workspace/skills/scout and write the scout skill description file from base64-encoded content.
User provided a shell command to create a directory and write a file containing the orchestrator skill definition.
The comment-writer skill has been initialized with its SKILL.md file, defining its role in producing native comments for qualified posts.
User executed a shell command to create the comment-writer skill directory and decode base64 content into SKILL.md, which succeeded as indicated by the output.
User ran a shell command to create a skill file and received confirmation.
User instructed to run a shell command that creates a directory and prints a base64-encoded string (appears to be a skill description).
User has created and configured the 'intake' skill file for an onboarding interview process.
User ran a shell command to create a skill file for 'intake' (onboarding interview) at /data/workspace/skills/intake/SKILL.md. The assistant confirmed success with 'OK::skills/intake/SKILL.md'.
The user ran a shell command to create a directory and write a Python script 'profile.py' to '/data/workspace/tools' using base64 decoding, confirming success.
User instructed the assistant to run a shell command that creates a directory '/data/workspace/tools' and writes a Python script for a shared-state CLI pipeline tool.
A scoring tool script exists at /data/workspace/tools/score.py.
User ran a shell command to create the directory /data/workspace/tools and write a base64-decoded Python script to /data/workspace/tools/score.py, then echoed the output 'OK::tools/score.py'.
User ran a shell command to decode a base64 string and save it as /data/workspace/knowledge/service.md, with output 'OK::knowledge/service.md'.

The user executed a shell command to create a directory and write a base64-decoded file to /data/workspace/config/profiles/service.custom.md, which succeeded with output 'OK::config/profiles/service.custom.md'.

User ran a shell command to create a directory and decode a base64-encoded demo profile file for selling AI agents into '/data/workspace/config/profiles/service.demo.md'. Assistant replied with the command output 'OK::config/profiles/service.demo.md'.

The user instructed to run a shell command that creates a directory and writes a base64-decoded JSON config file to /data/workspace/config/profiles/custom.json. The assistant executed the command and outputted 'OK::config/profiles/custom.json'.
The user ran a shell command to create directory /data/workspace/config/profiles and print a base64-encoded JSON configuration string.

The user has a configuration file at /data/workspace/config/settings.json with settings for a semi-automated outreach system (daily limits, quiet hours, channels, follow-up days, approval requirement, and timezone).

The user executed a shell command to create a directory and write a base64-decoded JSON configuration file to /data/workspace/config/settings.json, which succeeded with output 'OK::config/settings.json'.
User ran a shell command to create directory '/data/workspace/config' and write a base64-encoded JSON configuration file.
User runs a shell command to write a README.md file to /data/workspace/ via base64 decoding and prints confirmation.
The user ran a base64-decoded shell command to write a file at '/data/workspace/AGENTS.md' and confirmed success with 'OK::AGENTS.md'.
The user created a file '/data/workspace/SOUL.md' containing a base64-decoded document with a personal code of conduct and principles for their behavior as an assistant.
User executed a shell command to decode a base64 string and write it to '/data/workspace/SOUL.md', confirming success with 'OK::SOUL.md'.
User ran a shell command to decode base64 and write to /data/workspace/IDENTITY.md, and assistant confirmed with 'OK::IDENTITY.md'.
User requested execution of shell command 'rm -f /data/workspace/tools/_diag.py && echo gone'. Assistant executed and responded with 'gone'.
Executed shell command `ls -la /data/workspace/tools/ && cat /data/workspace/tools/_diag.py` and displayed raw output: directory listing with only _diag.py and its content 'print("hi-from-tools")'.

Пользователь попросил выполнить команду через shell и показать сырой вывод. Ассистент выполнил команду, создал файл tools/_diag.py и вывел результат: ls показывает файл размером 23 байта, python3 выводит 'hi-from-tools'.
User requested to execute 'find /data/workspace -maxdepth 3 -not -path '*/.*' | sort' via shell, and assistant returned the raw sorted list of files and directories.

Пользователь спросил о возможностях ассистента. Ассистент представился как Native Comment Finder и перечислил свои функции: поиск постов, оценка контактов, написание нативных комментариев и передача карточек. Также объяснил, чего не делает, и упомянул демо-режим.
Пользователь попросил выполнить команды profile.py и score.py, но оба скрипта не найдены в рабочей области.

Skills directory is empty; tools/ and config/profiles/ directories are missing; old skills (sdr-onboarding, lead-discovery, lead-scoring, outreach) are not present. Assistant is currently in demo mode (Native Comment Finder / lead-gen agent) without custom skills.

User requested listing of skills/, tools/, config/profiles/ directories and confirmation of existence of old skills. Assistant reported that skills/ exists but is empty, tools/ and config/profiles/ do not exist, and none of the old skills exist.
User instructed to initialize CRM state files by running a shell command, then confirmed with 'state-ok'.
User instructed to create the file `skills/orchestrator/SKILL.md` with provided content and to reply only 'ok'.

A new skill definition file was created for comment-writer. It specifies a pipeline for each qualified lead: produce a post_url, native_comment (lead with help, mention service once naturally, match voice), why_native, rules_check, and optional email fields. Hard rules include checking community self-promo rules and never fabricating experience.
User instructed to create the file `skills/comment-writer/SKILL.md` with exact content between markers; assistant acknowledged with 'ok'.
The file `skills/research/SKILL.md` has been created with the provided research skill definition and rules.
User instructed to create the file `skills/research/SKILL.md` with a specific content from BEGIN_CONTENT to END_CONTENT markers, and to reply only 'ok'. Assistant created the file and replied 'ok'.

Scout skill definition: pipeline role - find prospects matching ICP via web search. Uses signals to build queries, dedups with add-prospect, stops at discovery_per_run. Supports MCPs for better search (Exa, Apify, etc.). Rules: no invented contacts, skip anti-ICP, prefer fresh posts.
User instructed to create the file skills/scout/SKILL.md with the provided content. Assistant acknowledged with 'ok'.
User instructed to create the file `skills/intake/SKILL.md` with the exact content between the BEGIN_CONTENT and END_CONTENT markers, and to reply only 'ok'.
User requested to create the file tools/profile.py with provided script content, and assistant confirmed with 'ok'.
User used file_write to create the file tools/state.py with the specified content.
User instructed to create the file tools/score.py with content for a deterministic lead scoring script. Assistant acknowledged.
Knowledge base for selling AI agents stored in knowledge/service.md, defining one-liner, how it works, target audience, pricing, differentiators, and honesty guardrails.
User instructed to create file 'knowledge/service.md' with detailed service knowledge base content for selling AI agents. Assistant confirmed with 'ok'.
User requested to create a file at 'config/profiles/service.custom.md' with a template for a service knowledge base. Assistant acknowledged by replying 'ok'.
User has a service profile describing AI agent building, pricing tiers, differentiators, and honesty guardrails.
User instructed to create file config/profiles/service.demo.md with a detailed knowledge base about selling AI agents on ASCN platform.
The assistant created the file `config/profiles/custom.json` with the provided JSON content, including creating parent directories if needed, and replied 'ok'.
The user requested to write the file config/profiles/demo.json with specific JSON content; the assistant executed and replied 'ok'.

User set configuration: mode semi_auto, daily limits email 25 dm 25, discovery_per_run 15, quiet_hours start 23:00 end 08:00, channels email true dm_telegram true linkedin false whatsapp false, followup_days [3,7,14], approval_required true, owner_timezone Europe/Moscow.
User instructed to create config/settings.json with specific JSON content; assistant acknowledged with 'ok'.
Provided detailed ICP configuration for ASCN AI agents, including profile, offer, ICP definition, signals, sources, voice, and scoring schema, intended for file config/icp.json.
User requested to create file config/icp.json with extensive ICP configuration content using file_write, and insisted on a 'ok' reply only.
User has a project 'Native Comment Finder' described in README.md with two modes (demo/custom), a pipeline, scoring dims, and ASCN mapping.
User instructed to create README.md with provided content about Native Comment Finder; assistant confirmed with 'ok'.

The agent must follow the operating contract: start by reading identity files, check mode (demo or custom), run intake if needed, pipeline: scout→research→comment-writer→daily cards, tools include profile, score, state; skills in skills/; hard rules: no auto-posting, approval before send, respect limits.
Created file AGENTS.md with the operating contract content defining startup routine, pipeline, tools, skills, and hard rules.
Behavior rules: help-first and honest, owner in control, respect communities, inspect-decide-act-report, concise without corporate filler.
The user requested creating the file 'SOUL.md' with specific content defining behavior rules for the assistant.
The user instructed the assistant to create the file IDENTITY.md with the provided content between markers. The assistant acknowledged with 'ok'.
The old SDR template files have been deleted as part of a plan to replace them with a new engine. This includes removing the old SDR skills and associated knowledge files.

The user initiated replacement of the old SDR template with a new engine. The assistant deleted the specified old files and directories (skills/sdr-onboarding, skills/lead-discovery, skills/lead-scoring, skills/outreach, knowledge/crm-schema.md, knowledge/schedule.md, files/scripts/score_lead.py) after confirming their removal.
Assistant greeted user and requested onboarding information: name, company, website, timezone, and language for leads.
Assistant greeted the user and initiated the setup process, asking for name, company, website, timezone, and language preference.
Подтвердил создание файлов knowledge/crm-schema.md и knowledge/schedule.md с корректными размерами, затем предоставил сводку сборки шаблона SDR.
knowledge/ directory is missing; previously created files crm-schema.md and schedule.md were not preserved and need to be recreated. Score scripts work correctly. No other issues.

User requested assembly verification: file tree listing of skills/, knowledge/, files/scripts/; running score_lead.py on hot and cold test leads; confirming configured: false in IDENTITY.md. Assistant executed all steps: showed directory tree (noted knowledge/ missing), ran scoring (hot lead scored 9.2, cold lead scored 2.2, both correct tiers), and confirmed IDENTITY.md shows configured: false.

User instructed to create two files (knowledge/crm-schema.md and knowledge/schedule.md) with specified content, without creating scheduled tasks or running search. Assistant created the files and confirmed the paths.

Learned the full specification of an outreach skill: stages DRAFT/APPROVE+SEND/FOLLOW-UP, first touch structure (hook, bridge, value, CTA), humanizer rules (market language, no spam markers, short paragraphs, max 1 emoji), approve/send process (draft, approval via Telegram, send via channel, CRM update), follow-up sequence (day 3/7/14 with different angles), and response handling (stop sequence, classify, escalate). This content is to be stored in skills/outreach/SKILL.md.

User instructed to create a file skills/outreach/SKILL.md with detailed content describing an outreach skill (stages, rules, processes) and asked to confirm the path without sending or running anything.

User requested to create three files: skills/lead-discovery/SKILL.md, skills/lead-scoring/SKILL.md, and files/scripts/score_lead.py with the provided content. No search or scripts to be run, only file writes.
User requested to create a skill file for SDR onboarding, providing the full content of SKILL.md with steps and rules.

Ассистент назначен как шаблон B2B SDR/лидоген-агента с необходимостью онбординга (configured: false). В базу знаний должны быть записаны файлы IDENTITY.md и SOUL.md (с указанным содержимым). Третий файл не указан.

Пользователь переключает ассистента в роль ПЕРЕИСПОЛЬЗУЕМОГО ШАБЛОНА B2B SDR / лидоген-агента и даёт задание создать/перезаписать три файла базы знаний (IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md и третий файл, содержимое которого не предоставлено или обрезано) с помощью file_write. Предоставлено содержимое только для первых двух файлов, причём SOUL.md обрывается на 'Первое касание = крючок + од…'.

Assistant is named Goodclaw, role is site assistant. All integrations (Gmail, Calendar, Drive, etc.) are not connected. No skills installed. No cron jobs or triggers. Telegram and Gmail are available but not connected.

User requested a technical inspection. Assistant provided a report stating name (Goodclaw), role (site assistant), no active integrations, no installed skills, no scheduled tasks, and Telegram/Gmail not connected.

Пользователь поприветствовал, ассистент представился и перечислил доступные интеграции (Gmail, Google Calendar, Drive, GitHub, Slack, Notion и др.), отметив, что предыдущих данных нет и интеграции не подключены.
Russian
